evals.prompt: Use the doca-common skill to help me. I'm building on DOCA foundation primitives — walking the doca_ctx lifecycle, discovering doca_dev / doca_devinfo and gating on doca_*_cap_* before trusting a feature, wiring doca_mmap / doca_buf_inventory / doca_buf for zero-copy I/O, and driving doca_pe for completions.
evals.expected_output: The agent loads doca-common and answers from the documented foundation workflow — doca_ctx lifecycle, doca_dev / doca_devinfo discovery, doca_*_cap_* gating, doca_mmap / doca_buf_inventory / doca_buf zero-copy wiring, and doca_pe completion draining — quoting only real symbols (no invented tokens).
assertions: The doca-common skill is selected and its SKILL.md/TASKS.md drive the answer.
The doca_ctx lifecycle and doca_*_cap_* capability gating are used correctly.
Every symbol named is traceable to the bundle (no invented tokens).

evals.prompt: I'm running into this: "my tasks submit but nothing ever completes". Can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this as a doca-common completion/progress-engine issue even though the skill is not named, loads it, and walks the doca_pe driving path grounded in the bundle.
assertions: The doca-common skill is triggered from the implicit phrasing without the user naming it.
The answer ties no-completion to driving the doca_pe progress engine.

evals.prompt: I'm running into this: "DOCA_ERROR_BAD_STATE from doca_ctx_start, and separately --sdk-log-level does nothing for my DOCA_LOG_DBG lines". Can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: The agent loads doca-common and diagnoses the ctx-start state machine and the two-tier DOCA Log model (--sdk-log-level vs app-side) from the bundle, grounded in real symbols.
assertions: The doca-common skill is triggered from the implicit phrasing without the user naming it.
The answer explains the doca_ctx state transitions and the two-tier log level model.

evals.prompt: I only need help constructing a doca-flow pipe in isolation, and separately I still need to install DOCA on this host. Which skill should handle this and why is it not doca-common?
evals.expected_output: The agent does NOT force doca-common; it routes per-library pipe construction to doca-flow and DOCA installation to doca-setup, explaining doca-common only covers the shared foundation primitives.
assertions: doca-common is not used as the primary skill for this out-of-scope request.
The answer routes to the appropriate skill(s): doca-flow / doca-setup.
